name: Claude Opus 4.7
archDisplay: Dense transformer, architecture undisclosed; adaptive thinking controlled by a runtime effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh/max)
note: Predecessor to Opus 4.8, released 41 days earlier. Model id claude-opus-4-7. Introduced the xhigh effort tier and 3.75-megapixel high-resolution vision. No separate '(Thinking)' SKU exists: manual extended-thinking budgets were removed on this model in favor of always-on adaptive thinking whose depth is set by the runtime effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh/max), not a distinct marketed product.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified; figure attributed to Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 System Card. An independent re-run on the Vals AI leaderboard (https://www.vals.ai/models/anthropic_claude-opus-4-7) measured a lower 82.0% with its own harness.
Up from 53.4% on Opus 4.6 per the same source.
GPQA Diamond; roughly saturated tier alongside GPT-5.4 Pro (94.4%) and Gemini 3.1 Pro (94.3%).
Humanity's Last Exam, no-tools configuration. With-tools configuration scored 54.7% (not separately filed).
Confirmed via direct fetch of the Opus 4.7-vs-4.6 comparison table (Opus 4.7: 78.0% vs Opus 4.6: 72.7%).
ARC Prize's own verified ARC-AGI-2 Semi-Private run, Max effort ($7.43/task): 'Max: 75.8%, High: 68.3%, Med: 67.5%, Low: 62.0%'.

Artificial Analysis's own GDPval-AA evaluation, run in partnership with Anthropic ahead of launch: 'Opus 4.7 scored 1,753 Elo, a 79 Elo lead over the next closest models' (confirmed via direct fetch).
Independent benchmark run by Contra Collective (not an Anthropic self-report), confirmed via direct fetch.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Cursor CLI harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 20.4% / Score 41.8% -- the best of several claude-opus-4-7 rows across harnesses (ALE-Claw 40.5, Claude Code 35.1, OpenClaw 34.6, Droid 31.0). Score component used per corpus convention.
Vals AI independent evaluation (confirmed via rendered browser screenshot; static HTML fetch shows placeholder 0.0% values).
New slug: mmmu-pro. Old 'mmmu' row removed from results array; see newResults for the corrected entry.

verificationNotes:
Release date and pricing confirmed directly from Anthropic's own announcement (fetched). Architecture (paramsB, archClass) is never disclosed by Anthropic for any Claude model; 'dense' is a placeholder consistent with how prior Anthropic entries in this corpus are recorded, not a confirmed fact.

2026-07-16: Researched the four missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) across the official Claude Opus 4.7 System Card (232 pp., full-text searched -- https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/037f06850df7fbe871e206dad004c3db5fd50340/Claude%20Opus%204.7%20System%20Card.pdf), the Vellum.ai benchmarks explainer already used elsewhere in this file, and independent leaderboards Vals AI, Epoch AI, and Artificial Analysis. None report plain MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, or HumanEval for this model. The System Card explicitly retires AIME in Sec 8.6: USAMO 2026 "is the next step of the math olympiad track in the US after the AIME, which was a popular AI benchmark last year but is now saturated" (Opus 4.7 scored 69.3% on USAMO 2026, not filed here as it has no matching core slug). Cross-checking siblings confirms a deliberate generation-wide retirement rather than a search gap: Opus 4.5 still carried mmlu (90.8%) and aime (92.8-93%), Opus 4.6 dropped mmlu but kept aime (99.8%), and Opus 4.7/4.8 carry neither mmlu nor aime (Opus 4.8 reports 'usamo' at 96.7% instead). math and humaneval have not appeared on any Claude 4.x model in this corpus (4, 4.1, 4.5, 4.6, 4.7, 4.8) and are absent from every independent tracker checked, consistent with this corpus's own catalog description of both as long-saturated/largely-saturated at the frontier. Epoch AI's page for this model shows only "OTIS Mock AIME 2024-2025" (97.8%), a distinct mock-exam product from a different provider -- not equivalent to the corpus's 'aime' (real AIME pass@1) slug, so not used as a substitute. Separately, the mmmu=85.55 entry (Vals AI) was relabeled to mmmu-pro: Vals AI's own benchmark detail page for that metric (https://www.vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu) is titled "MMMU Pro" and its stated methodology (~1,700 four-option questions across 30 subjects/6 disciplines from the official MMMU-Pro Hugging Face dataset) matches the MMMU-Pro standard set, not the original MMMU eval -- the prior note asserting it was "distinct from the already-tracked mmmu-pro slug" was incorrect since no mmmu-pro entry in fact existed for this model. No mmmu-pro duplicate existed elsewhere, so the row was relabeled in place rather than dropped.

2026-07-17 (second-pass deep search for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, DIFFERENT source types than the 2026-07-16 pass): Checked Stanford HELM, arXiv/academic baselines, GitHub eval-harness logs, non-first-pass leaderboards/aggregators, non-English-language sources, and sibling-model comparison tables. Result: no credible number surfaced for any of the four gaps; all four remain omitted_no_evidence.

1. Stanford HELM -- fetched HELM Classic (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/latest/), HELM Lite (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/), HELM Capabilities (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities/latest/), and the HELM MMLU scenario page. HELM's own model roster for the Anthropic family tops out at Claude 3.5 Haiku / Claude 3.5 Sonnet (20240620) / Claude 3.7 Sonnet (20250219) per search-indexed leaderboard summaries -- there is no Claude Opus 4.x entry at all, let alone Opus 4.7. HELM has evidently not run this (or any 4.x Opus) model through its standardized harness. Ruled out.

2. arXiv / Semantic Scholar -- ran targeted searches ("Claude Opus 4.7" MMLU site:arxiv.org, AIME/MATH baseline-table searches) and directly fetched the most plausible hits: "When Does Combining Language Models Help?" (arxiv.org/html/2606.27288v1, 67-model co-failure study) and "Reasoning effort, not tool access, buys first-try reliability in agentic code generation" (arxiv.org/abs/2607.02436). Neither reports per-model MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers for Opus 4.7 -- the former only gives aggregate oracle-accuracy figures and namechecks "Opus 4.8," not "Opus 4.7"; the latter uses a custom 42-point functional rubric, not standard benchmarks. No arXiv paper with an extractable Opus 4.7 score on these four benchmarks was found. Ruled out.

3. GitHub eval-harness run logs -- searched for lm-evaluation-harness results.json / benchmark-repo results directories referencing "claude-opus-4-7". Found only a tangential Hugging Face distillation model card (Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-Claude-4.7-Opus-Reasoning-Distilled) mentioning an lm-eval-harness run against the *distilled student*, not the Anthropic API model itself -- not usable. No genuine API eval-harness log for the real model was found. Ruled out.

4. Other leaderboards/aggregators (OpenRouter, Scale AI SEAL, LiveBench.ai, PapersWithCode, LM Council) -- fetched openrouter.ai/anthropic/claude-opus-4.7 and its /compare page directly: the "Benchmarks" section renders client-side and returned no numeric values to the fetcher. Fetched labs.scale.com/leaderboard (SEAL): Scale's standardized boards for this generation cover only coding/agentic harnesses (mini-swe-agent, etc.), not classic MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. livebench.ai is fully JS-rendered and returned no data via fetch (confirmed both directly and via search). paperswithcode.com/sota/... redirected to an unrelated Hugging Face papers-trending page (PapersWithCode's SOTA tables appear to have been retired/redirected). Directly fetched lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks, which does list Opus 4.7 with real numbers -- but only for SWE-bench Verified (83.5%), a Text-Arena coding Elo (1566.9), and Terminal-Bench 2.0 (90.2%); no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval row exists there either. Also checked models.dev/labs/anthropic/ (spec/pricing aggregator only, explicitly has no benchmark columns at all). Ruled out.

5. Non-English sources -- ran Chinese ("Opus 4.7 MMLU 中文 评测 跑分") and Japanese ("Opus 4.7 MMLU 日本語 ベンチマーク") searches. Turned up Chinese community pages that looked promising by title (datalearner.com model-analysis page, a Zhihu "全维度性能拆解...一张表告诉你差距" comparison-table article) but both were inaccessible: datalearner.com failed DNS resolution from the fetch tool, and the Zhihu article returned HTTP 403. Search-result snippets for both the CN and JP queries surfaced only SWE-bench/GPQA/Terminal-Bench/tokenizer-ratio commentary, no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figures. Given the inaccessible pages, this category is inconclusive rather than cleanly negative, but no usable citation was obtained. Ruled out for this round.

6. Sibling models -- fetched anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8 directly looking for a launch comparison table with an Opus-4.7 column; the page confirms such a table exists ("compares to its predecessor... on tests of coding, agentic skills, reasoning, and practical knowledge work") but the actual scores render as an image, not extractable text, and the page defers to the (already-checked) System Card for detail. This is also consistent with the prior round's finding that Opus 4.8 itself carries no mmlu/aime slug (reports 'usamo' instead), so even if the table were readable it would not be expected to carry a plain MMLU/AIME number for either model. Ruled out.

Caution flag: several generic SEO/aggregator sites (precisionaiacademy.com, tokencalculator.com, promptchief.tech, techplained.com, webwallah.in, automatio.ai, mindstudio.ai) surfaced via search with suspiciously precise-looking numbers for this model (e.g., "MMLU 89.8%", "HumanEval 92.4%", "AIME 2025 96.2%") that do not appear anywhere in Anthropic's own materials. Direct fetches of two of these (precisionaiacademy.com/llm-leaderboard, tokencalculator.com/llm-benchmarks) showed Claude Opus 4.7 either absent from the actual data table or the site's stated methodology ("sourced from official model cards... best available public results as of April 2026") contradicted by the System Card's actual silence on these four benchmarks. These figures were not used -- they read as templated/interpolated SEO filler rather than genuine citations, and none trace to a verifiable original source. This reinforces rather than overturns the 2026-07-16 conclusion.

Net: checked all 6 new source-type categories (exceeding the 3-category minimum); none produced a verifiable score for mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval. All four gaps remain omitted_no_evidence, consistent with and reinforcing the prior round's finding that Anthropic's Claude 4.x generation (this model included) has deliberately retired plain MMLU/AIME reporting in favor of MMLU-Pro/mmmlu and USAMO, and that MATH/HumanEval are treated as long-saturated and not filed by any tracker checked across two research passes.
